summary: Phase O.6 'Eyes Single-Pass' pre-deployment safety check completed. Verified LEGACY multi-pass pipeline (EYES_ONE_PASS=false) remains fully functional with zero behavioral change. All backend APIs work correctly, frontend ItemDetail page renders without ReferenceErrors, and both Clean Background and Repair photo CTAs are properly wired.

design_issues.issue: React hydration warning: nested <a> tags in closet item cards (LinkWithRef wrapping an <a>)
design_issues.note: Pre-existing issue, not related to Phase O.6 changes

passed_tests: Backend: POST /api/v1/closet/analyze with test image succeeds (legacy multi-pass, no one_pass flag)
Backend: GET /api/v1/closet/{id} returns item with correct schema (clean_image_status field present)
Backend: POST /api/v1/closet/{id}/repair endpoint exists (returns 422 for no image, not 404)
Frontend: Login flow works with dev@dressapp.io credentials
Frontend: Closet page loads with items grid
Frontend: ItemDetail page loads without ReferenceErrors (variable rename successful)
Frontend: Clean Background button (data-testid='item-clean-bg-button') is present and clickable
Frontend: Clean Background button shows progress bar during operation
Frontend: Clean Background operation completes successfully
Frontend: Main image renders correctly using bestImageUrl fallback logic
Frontend: Repair photo button (Phase O.6 CTA) is conditionally rendered (only when reconstruction_advised=true)

notes: EYES_ONE_PASS flag is correctly defaulting to false (legacy path active)
No BackgroundTask is enqueued for rembg in legacy path (from_one_pass=false)
Clean Background CTA uses renamed state variables (cleaningBackground, cleanBackgroundProgress, onCleanBackground)
Repair photo CTA uses new state variables (reshootingPhoto, onReshootPhoto)
No orphan references to old variable names (repairing, repairProgress, onRepair) detected
bestImageUrl utility correctly resolves image fallbacks in expected order
All closet CRUD operations work as expected
The only console errors are pre-existing React hydration warnings unrelated to Phase O.6 changes
